channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Textbook titan McGraw Hill on ransomware crew's reading list after 13.5M records exposed
item.description: McGraw Hill has been implicated in a data breach that exposed 13.5 million records due to a misconfigured Salesforce page, landing it on a ransomware group's leak site. This incident highlights vulnerabilities in data security practices among major companies.

item.title: This Beanie Is Designed to Read Your Thoughts
item.description: Startup Sabi is creating a wearable device that translates thoughts into text, potentially revolutionizing communication technology. This innovation could mark a significant step towards integrating human cognition with technology.

item.title: Swarm welcome: Britain lines up 120,000 drones for Ukraine
item.description: The UK government plans to supply Ukraine with 120,000 drones, including various types for military support, as part of its ongoing assistance in the conflict against Russia. This move signifies a major escalation in military aid.

item.title: Microsoft announces product it doesn't want you to buy: Extended security updates for old Exchange, and Skype for Biz
item.description: Microsoft will continue offering extended security updates for outdated versions of Exchange and Skype for Business, acknowledging that some customers are not ready to upgrade. This decision underscores the challenges businesses face in transitioning to new software.

item.title: This stroller turns into a carry on-suitcase, and I recommend it for traveling parents
item.description: The TernX stroller, which converts into a carry-on suitcase, aims to simplify travel for parents. The article evaluates its practicality and high cost for potential buyers.

item.title: The noise we make is hurting animals. Can we learn to shut up?
item.description: The article discusses how reduced noise during the COVID-19 pandemic allowed for a resurgence in animal sounds, prompting a reflection on the impact of human noise pollution on wildlife. It emphasizes the need for quieter environments for ecological health.

item.title: The quest to measure our relationship with nature
item.description: The piece explores a shift in environmentalism towards recognizing the positive role humans can play in nature conservation. It highlights practices like Indigenous burning to prevent wildfires and the importance of integrating human efforts with ecological preservation.

item.title: Is carbon removal in trouble?
item.description: Reports indicate that Microsoft is pausing its carbon removal purchases, which could destabilize the carbon removal market as the company has been a dominant buyer. This raises concerns about the future of carbon offset initiatives.

item.title: Obsolete Google nag drowns out vital bar information at Swedish concert hall
item.description: An obsolete Google application caused disruptions at a concert hall in Sweden, overshadowing important announcements. This incident illustrates the ongoing challenges of managing outdated technology in public settings.

item.title: The best small business VoIP providers of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: The article reviews various VoIP providers for small businesses, assessing their features and value. It aims to guide businesses in selecting the best communication solutions.

item.title: Protect your devices with our pick for the best antivirus software, now over 60% off
item.description: Bitdefender Total Security is currently over 60% off on Amazon, providing users with protection against spam and malware. This significant discount makes it an attractive option for consumers looking to enhance their cybersecurity.

item.title: Cops hand Motorola £25M no-bid deal to keep 2000-era radios alive
item.description: UK police have awarded Motorola a £25 million no-bid contract to maintain outdated communications technology, highlighting issues with the delayed and over-budget Emergency Services Network replacement project. This raises concerns about public sector procurement practices.

item.title: Server-room lock was nothing but a crock
item.description: A recent column discusses the importance of physical security for server rooms, illustrating how poor practices can lead to significant vulnerabilities. The article emphasizes that cybersecurity measures are only as strong as the physical protections in place.

item.title: DeepL, known for text translation, now wants to translate your voice
item.description: DeepL is expanding its capabilities from text translation to voice translation, aiming to integrate real-time translation features into platforms like Zoom and Microsoft Teams. This advancement could enhance communication in multilingual settings.

item.title: Accelerating the cyber defense ecosystem that protects us all
item.description: OpenAI's Trusted Access for Cyber initiative has gained support from leading security firms and enterprises, utilizing GPT-5.4-Cyber and $10 million in API grants to bolster global cybersecurity efforts. This collaboration aims to enhance the cyber defense ecosystem.

item.title: Why many Americans are turning to AI for health advice, according to recent polls
item.description: A recent poll indicates that many Americans are increasingly relying on AI tools for health advice. This trend reflects a growing acceptance of AI in personal health management.

item.title: It’s Wednesday. Do You Own Enough Google?
item.description: An investor reflects on their long-term experience with Alphabet (Google), noting the company's resilience through various challenges. This personal account highlights the volatility and potential of tech investments.

item.title: enact-langchain 0.1.2
item.description: The release of enact-langchain 0.1.2 introduces new tools for the ENACT Protocol, which focuses on trustless escrow for AI agents on the TON blockchain. This development could enhance the functionality of AI in decentralized applications.

item.title: NVIDIA's new Ising AI could finally make quantum computing usable
item.description: NVIDIA has launched Ising, a new family of open-source AI models aimed at making quantum computing more practical. This development could significantly impact the future of quantum technologies.

item.title: Gupshup Launches Superagent: the Autonomous AI Agent for Customer Conversations at Scale
item.description: Gupshup has introduced Superagent, an autonomous AI agent designed to handle customer conversations at scale. This innovation aims to streamline customer service operations and improve user engagement.

item.title: elitea-sdk 0.5.24
item.description: EliteA has released version 0.5.24 of its SDK, which enables developers to build LangChain agents using its resources. This update enhances the capabilities for creating AI-driven applications.

item.title: aigility 0.1.0
item.description: Aigility 0.1.0 is a new Python Agent Development Kit that provides a type-safe framework for creating AI agents with features like chat and workflow management. This toolkit aims to streamline the development process for intelligent agents.

item.title: Did Anthropic Just Crown CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks the AI Cybersecurity Stock Winners?
item.description: The article discusses how Anthropic's recent moves may position CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks as leading AI cybersecurity stocks. The analysis highlights the growing importance of AI in cybersecurity.

item.title: Blaize Announces Expected First Quarter 2026 Revenue and Newly Awarded Contract with NeoTensr Anticipated to Generate $50.0 Million in Revenue
item.description: Blaize has announced expected revenue of $50 million for the first quarter of 2026, attributed to a newly awarded contract with NeoTensr. This significant contract reflects Blaize's growing influence in the AI market.

item.title: How This Open-Source AI Agent Actually Learns from Its Own Mistakes : Hermes Agent
item.description: The Hermes Agent, developed by Nous Research, is an innovative AI system that learns from its mistakes through a unique adaptation mechanism. This continuous learning capability enhances its performance over time.

item.title: Show HN: Chrome extension that extracts styles and generates DESIGN.md files
item.description: A new Chrome extension allows users to extract styles from web pages and generate DESIGN.md files. This tool aims to streamline the design documentation process for developers.

item.title: Buzzy vibe-coding startup Emergent is launching an AI agent to take on OpenClaw and NanoBot
item.description: Emergent is launching Wingman, a personal AI agent designed to operate on messaging platforms like WhatsApp and iMessage, positioning itself against competitors like OpenClaw and NanoBot. This move reflects the growing trend of AI in personal communication.

item.title: enact-langchain 0.1.1
item.description: Enact-langchain 0.1.1 introduces tools for the ENACT Protocol, which provides trustless escrow services for AI agents on the TON blockchain. This integration aims to enhance security and trust in AI transactions.

item.title: langchain-couchbase 1.0.2
item.description: Langchain-couchbase 1.0.2 is an integration package that connects Couchbase with LangChain, facilitating the development of applications that utilize both technologies. This integration aims to enhance data management for AI applications.

item.title: tvplot added to PyPI
item.description: Tvplot has been added to PyPI, allowing users to extract plotlines from TV series synopses using large language models. This tool aims to assist in content analysis and summarization.

item.title: axiom-calculator-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: A new personal finance calculator package, axiom-calculator-mcp, has been added to PyPI, allowing AI agents to perform various financial calculations without needing API keys.

item.title: Show HN: Visibl – turns audiobooks into graphic novels
item.description: Visibl is a new project that transforms audiobooks into graphic novels, merging storytelling formats for enhanced engagement.

item.title: Solaria Eyes Stake in €4 Billion Telefónica, ACS Data Center JV
item.description: Solaria is negotiating to join a €4 billion data center project in Spain, highlighting efforts to bolster digital infrastructure in Europe.

item.title: Silicon Valley has no monopoly on AI brain power. That’s why Demis Hassabis is very happy to stay in London
item.description: Demis Hassabis asserts that London can rival Silicon Valley in AI talent, emphasizing that innovation is not limited to one geographic area.

item.title: WATCH: Principal stops alleged potential school shooter on surveillance video
item.description: A principal intervened to stop a potential school shooter, showcasing quick action against a serious threat captured on surveillance.

item.title: mind-mem 3.1.1
item.description: The release of mind-mem 3.1.1 provides a drop-in memory solution for Claude Code and other compatible agents, enhancing their functionality.

item.title: Show HN: SynapseKit – Async-native Python framework for LLM pipelines and agents
item.description: SynapseKit is introduced as an async-native Python framework for building LLM applications, designed for simplicity and efficiency.

item.title: hol-guard 2.0.12
item.description: HOL Guard 2.0.12 is now available, providing protection for local AI harnesses before executing tasks in various AI environments.

item.title: 14-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 24GB 1TB Is $149 Off
item.description: The 14-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro, designed for demanding AI workloads, is now available at a discounted price, showcasing its capabilities for LLM training.

item.title: AI Is Writing Our Code Faster Than We Can Verify It
item.description: The article discusses the rapid pace of AI writing code, outstripping human verification capabilities, raising concerns about quality and oversight.

item.title: cyllama-vulkan 0.2.8
item.description: Cyllama version 0.2.8 is released, offering a zero-dependencies Python library for local AI inference with llama, whisper, and stable-diffusion technologies. This update enhances accessibility for developers working with AI models.

item.title: cyllama-sycl 0.2.8
item.description: Cyllama version 0.2.8 is released, providing a zero-dependencies Python library for local AI inference with llama, whisper, and stable-diffusion technologies. This update enhances accessibility for developers working with AI models.

item.title: neural-memory 4.49.0
item.description: Neural-memory version 4.49.0 introduces persistent memory for AI agents, featuring 56 MCP tools and neuroscience-inspired recall mechanisms. It supports popular AI models like Claude, GPT, and Gemini.

item.title: cyllama-rocm 0.2.8
item.description: Cyllama version 0.2.8 is released, offering a zero-dependencies Python library for local AI inference with llama, whisper, and stable-diffusion technologies. This update enhances accessibility for developers working with AI models.

item.title: DeepGPR 0.0.4
item.description: DeepGPR version 0.0.4 is released, utilizing PyTorch and CUDA for Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) Full Waveform Inversion (FWI). This tool aims to improve computational efficiency in AI applications.

item.title: cyllama-cuda12 0.2.8
item.description: Cyllama version 0.2.8 is released, offering a zero-dependencies Python library for local AI inference with llama, whisper, and stable-diffusion technologies. This update enhances accessibility for developers working with AI models.

item.title: Experience AI: Reaching millions of young people with AI literacy
item.description: Experience AI is a global education program launched in collaboration with Google DeepMind, aimed at teaching young people about AI's benefits and risks. This initiative recognizes the importance of AI literacy in today's world.

item.title: Android Auto users say Gemini won’t stop talking, and it’s not even getting things right
item.description: Users of Android Auto report issues with Gemini, stating it frequently talks without providing accurate information. This raises concerns about the effectiveness of AI integration in automotive systems.

item.title: U.S and Iran could renew peace talks and new allegations made against Swalwell: Morning Rundown
item.description: The U.S. and Iran may resume peace talks, indicating a potential shift in diplomatic relations. This development could have significant implications for international politics.

item.title: i6-models 1.20260415.125454
item.description: i6-models version 1.20260415.125454 is released, featuring a collection of PyTorch neural network models and components. This resource is valuable for developers working on AI projects.
